{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is about the cells most commonly affected in glaucomatous optic atrophy. Reviewing the documents, Document [4] (Title: Glaucoma -- Introduction) states 'Glaucoma is an acquired loss of retinal ganglion cells and axons within the optic nerve or optic neuropathy that results in a characteristic optic nerve head appearance and a corresponding progressive loss of vision.' This clearly indicates that ganglion cells are the primary cells affected in glaucomatous optic atrophy.", "answer_choice": "C"}
